info.description: Build custom integrations with the LaunchDarkly REST API
info.title: LaunchDarkly REST API

get.description: You can issue a GET request to the root resource to find all of the resource categories supported by the API.

get.summary: Returns a list of relay proxy configurations in the account.

name.description: A human-friendly name for the relay proxy configuration

requestBody.description: Create a new relay proxy configuration

post.summary: Create a new relay proxy config.

delete.summary: Delete a relay proxy configuration by ID.

get.summary: Get a single relay proxy configuration by ID.

requestBody.description: Requires a JSON Patch representation of the desired changes to the project. 'http://jsonpatch.com/'

patch.summary: Modify a relay proxy configuration by ID.

post.summary: Reset a relay proxy configuration's secret key with an optional expiry time for the old key.

get.summary: Get a list of all audit log entries. The query parameters allow you to restrict the returned results by date ranges, resource specifiers, or a full-text search query.
Use this endpoint to fetch a single audit log entry by its resouce ID.
Returns a list of all data export destinations.

kind.description: The data export destination type. Available choices are kinesis, google-pubsub, mparticle, or segment.

name.description: A human-readable name for your data export destination.

true.description: Whether the data export destination is on or not.

requestBody.description: Create a new data export destination.

post.summary: Create a new data export destination

delete.summary: Get a single data export destination by ID

get.summary: Get a single data export destination by ID

requestBody.description: Requires a JSON Patch representation of the desired changes to the project. 'http://jsonpatch.com/' Feature flag patches also support JSON Merge Patch format. 'https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7386' The addition of comments is also supported.

patch.summary: Perform a partial update to a data export destination.

200.description: Status of the requested feature flag across environments

get.summary: Get the status for a particular feature flag across environments
Get a list of statuses for all feature flags. The status includes the last time the feature flag was requested, as well as the state of the flag.

200.description: Status of the requested feature flag.

get.summary: Get the status for a particular feature flag.
Get a list of all features in the given project.

description.description: A description of the feature flag.
description.example: This flag controls whether test feature is turned on or not.

includeInSnippet.description: Whether or not this flag should be made available to the client-side JavaScript SDK.

key.description: A unique key that will be used to reference the flag in your code.

name.description: A human-friendly name for the feature flag. Remember to note if this flag is intended to be temporary or permanent.

temporary.description: Whether or not the flag is a temporary flag.

variations.description: An array of possible variations for the flag.

requestBody.description: Create a new feature flag.

post.summary: Creates a new feature flag.

200.description: Dependent flags in a single envrionemnt response

get.summary: Get dependent flags for the flag in the environment specified in path parameters

delete.summary: Delete a feature flag in all environments. Be careful-- only delete feature flags that are no longer being used by your application.

get.summary: Get a single feature flag by key.

requestBody.description: Requires a JSON Patch representation of the desired changes to the project, and an optional comment. 'http://jsonpatch.com/' Feature flag patches also support JSON Merge Patch format. 'https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7386' The addition of comments is also supported.

patch.summary: Perform a partial update to a feature.

comment.description: comment will be included in audit log item for change.

excludedActions.description: Define the parts of the flag configuration that will not be copied.

includedActions.description: Define the parts of the flag configuration that will be copied.

requestBody.description: Copy feature flag configurations between environments.

post.summary: Copies the feature flag configuration from one environment to the same feature flag in another environment.

200.description: Dependent flags accross all environemnts in the project response

get.summary: Get dependent flags across all environments for the flag specified in the path parameters

200.description: User targets of the requested feature flag.

get.summary: Get expiring user targets for feature flag

200.description: User targeting expirations on feature flag response.

requestBody.description: Requires a Semantic Patch representation of the desired changes to the resource. 'https://apidocs.launchdarkly.com/reference#updates-via-semantic-patches'. The addition of comments is also supported.

patch.summary: Update, add, or delete expiring user targets on feature flag

get.summary: Get a list of all configured audit log event integrations associated with this account.
Get a list of all configured integrations of a given kind.

name.description: A human-readable name for your subscription configuration.

true.description: Whether the integration subscription is active or not.

requestBody.description: Create a new integration subscription.

post.summary: Create a new integration subscription of a given kind.

delete.summary: Delete an integration subscription by ID.

get.summary: Get a single integration subscription by ID.

requestBody.description: Requires a JSON Patch representation of the desired changes to the project. 'http://jsonpatch.com/'

patch.summary: Modify an integration subscription by ID.

get.summary: Returns a list of all members in the account.

items.description: The 20-hexdigit id or the key for a custom role.

requestBody.description: New members to invite.

post.summary: Invite new members.

get.summary: Get the current team member associated with the token

delete.summary: Delete a team member by ID.

get.summary: Get a single team member by ID.

requestBody.description: Requires a JSON Patch representation of the desired changes to the project. 'http://jsonpatch.com/'

patch.summary: Modify a team member by ID.

get.summary: Returns a list of all projects in the account.

requestBody.description: Project keys must be unique within an account.

post.summary: Create a new project with the given key and name.

delete.summary: Delete a project by key. Caution-- deleting a project will delete all associated environments and feature flags. You cannot delete the last project in an account.

get.summary: Fetch a single project by key.

requestBody.description: Requires a JSON Patch representation of the desired changes to the project. 'http://jsonpatch.com/'
New environment.

post.summary: Create a new environment in a specified project with a given name, key, and swatch color.

delete.summary: Delete an environment in a specific project.

get.summary: Get an environment given a project and key.

requestBody.description: Requires a JSON Patch representation of the desired changes to the project. 'http://jsonpatch.com/'

patch.summary: Modify an environment by ID. If you try to patch the environment by setting both required and requiredApprovalTags, it will result in an error. Users can specify either required approvals for all flags in an environment or those with specific tags, but not both. Only customers on an Enterprise plan can require approval for flag updates with either mechanism.

post.summary: Reset an environment's SDK key with an optional expiry time for the old key.
Reset an environment's mobile key. The optional expiry for the old key is deprecated for this endpoint, so the old key will always expire immediately.

get.summary: Get all approval requests for a feature flag config

comment.description: comment will be included in audit log item for change.

description.description: A name that describes the changes you would like to apply to a feature flag configuration

executionDate.description: Timestamp for when instructions will be executed

operatingOnId.description: ID of scheduled change to edit or delete

requestBody.description: Create a new approval request

delete.summary: Delete an approval request for a feature flag config

comment.description: comment will be included in audit log item for change.

executionDate.description: Timestamp for when instructions will be executed

operatingOnId.description: ID of scheduled change to edit or delete

post.summary: Create an approval request for a feature flag config

comment.description: comment will be included in audit log item for change.

requestBody.description: Apply an approval request

comment.description: comment will be included in audit log item for change.

kind.description: One of approve, decline, or comment.

requestBody.description: Review an approval request

200.description: A list of scheduled changes for feature flag response.

get.summary: Get all scheduled workflows for a feature flag by key.

201.description: A scheduled change for feature flag response.

comment.description: Used to describe the scheduled changes.

executionDate.description: A unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the date the scheduled changes will be applied

requestBody.description: Create scheduled changes on a feature flag.

post.summary: Creates a new scheduled change for a feature flag.

executionDate.description: A unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the date the scheduled changes will be applied

requestBody.description: Used to determine if a semantic patch will result in conflicts with scheduled changes on a feature flag.

post.summary: Lists conflicts between the given instructions and any existing scheduled changes for the feature flag. The actual HTTP verb should be REPORT, not POST.

delete.summary: Delete a scheduled change on a feature flag in an environment.

200.description: A scheduled change for feature flag response.

get.summary: Get a scheduled change on a feature flag by id.

201.description: A Scheduled change for feature flag response.

comment.description: Used to describe the scheduled changes.

requestBody.description: Update scheduled changes on a feature flag.

patch.summary: Updates an existing scheduled-change on a feature flag in an environment.

get.summary: Return a complete list of custom roles.

description.example: Description of revenue team role here

key.description: The 20-hexdigit id or the key for a custom role.

name.description: Name of the custom role.

requestBody.description: New role or roles to create.

post.summary: Create a new custom role.

delete.summary: Delete a custom role by key.

get.summary: Get one custom role by key.

requestBody.description: Requires a JSON Patch representation of the desired changes to the project. 'http://jsonpatch.com/'

patch.summary: Modify a custom role by key.

get.summary: Get a list of all user segments in the given project.

description.description: A description for the user segment.
description.example: Users in this segment will have access to beta features.

key.description: A unique key that will be used to reference the user segment in feature flags.

name.description: A human-friendly name for the user segment.

unbounded.description: Controls whether this is considered a "big segment" which can support an unlimited numbers of users. Include/exclude lists sent with this payload are not used in big segments. Contact your account manager for early access to this feature.

requestBody.description: Create a new user segment.

post.summary: Creates a new user segment.

get.summary: Get a single user segment by key.

requestBody.description: Requires a JSON Patch representation of the desired changes to the project. 'http://jsonpatch.com/' Feature flag patches also support JSON Merge Patch format. 'https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7386' The addition of comments is also supported.

patch.summary: Perform a partial update to a user segment.

requestBody.description: Add or remove user targets to the included or excluded lists on a big segment. Contact your account manager for early access to this feature.

post.summary: Update targets included or excluded in a big segment

200.description: User targeting expirations on user segment response.

get.summary: Get expiring user targets for user segment

200.description: User targeting expirations on user segment response.

requestBody.description: Requires a Semantic Patch representation of the desired changes to the resource. 'https://apidocs.launchdarkly.com/reference#updates-via-semantic-patches'. The addition of comments is also supported.

patch.summary: Update, add, or delete expiring user targets on user segment

get.summary: Returns a list of tokens in the account.

customRoleIds.description: A list of custom role IDs to use as access limits for the access token

defaultApiVersion.description: The default API version for this token

name.description: A human-friendly name for the access token

role.description: The name of a built-in role for the token

serviceToken.description: Whether the token will be a service token https://docs.launchdarkly.com/home/account-security/api-access-tokens#service-tokens

requestBody.description: Create a new access token.

post.summary: Create a new token.

delete.summary: Delete an access token by ID.

get.summary: Get a single access token by ID.

requestBody.description: Requires a JSON Patch representation of the desired changes to the project. 'http://jsonpatch.com/'

patch.summary: Modify an access token by ID.

post.summary: Reset an access token's secret key with an optional expiry time for the old key.

get.summary: Returns of the usage endpoints available.

200.description: Returns timeseries data and all sdk versions.

get.summary: Get events usage by event id and the feature flag key.

200.description: Returns timeseries data and all sdk versions.

get.summary: Get events usage endpoints.

200.description: Returns timeseries data and all sdk versions.

get.summary: Get events usage by event type.

200.description: Returns timeseries data and all sdk versions.

get.summary: Get monthly active user data.

200.description: Returns timeseries data and all sdk versions.

get.summary: Get monthly active user data by category.
Returns a list of all streams.

200.description: Responds with time series data on stream usage.

get.summary: Get a stream endpoint and return timeseries data.

200.description: Returns timeseries data and metadata on sdk version.

get.summary: Get a stream timeseries data by source show sdk version metadata.

200.description: Returns timeseries data and all sdk versions.

get.summary: Get a stream timeseries data by source and show all sdk version associated.
Search users in LaunchDarkly based on their last active date, or a search query. It should not be used to enumerate all users in LaunchDarkly-- use the List users API resource.

List all users in the environment. Includes the total count of users. In each page, there will be up to 'limit' users returned (default 20). This is useful for exporting all users in the system for further analysis. Paginated collections will include a next link containing a URL with the next set of elements in the collection.
Get a user by key.
Fetch a single flag setting for a user by key.
Fetch a single flag setting for a user by key.

setting.description: The variation value to set for the user. Must match the variation type of the flag.

put.summary: Specifically enable or disable a feature flag for a user based on their key.

200.description: User targets of the requested feature flag.

get.summary: Get expiring dates on flags for user

200.description: User Targeting expiration for single user on all flags response.

requestBody.description: Requires a Semantic Patch representation of the desired changes to the resource. 'https://apidocs.launchdarkly.com/reference#updates-via-semantic-patches'. The addition of comments is also supported.

patch.summary: Update, add, or delete expiring user targets for a single user on all flags

get.summary: Fetch a list of all webhooks.

name.description: The name of the webhook.

secret.description: If sign is true, and the secret attribute is omitted, LaunchDarkly will automatically generate a secret for you.

sign.description: If sign is false, the webhook will not include a signature header, and the secret can be omitted.

true.description: Whether this webhook is enabled or not.

url.description: The URL of the remote webhook.

requestBody.description: New webhook.

post.summary: Create a webhook.

get.summary: Get a webhook by ID.

requestBody.description: Requires a JSON Patch representation of the desired changes to the project. 'http://jsonpatch.com/'

After.description: A timestamp filter, expressed as a Unix epoch time in milliseconds. All entries returned will have occurred after this timestamp.
After.name: after

ArchivedQuery.description: When set to 1, only archived flags will be included in the list of flags returned.  By default, archived flags are not included in the list of flags.
ArchivedQuery.name: archived

AuditLimit.description: A limit on the number of audit log entries to be returned, between 1 and 20.
AuditLimit.name: limit

Before.description: A timestamp filter, expressed as a Unix epoch time in milliseconds. All entries returned will have before this timestamp.
Before.name: before

CloneFlagKeyQuery.description: The key of the feature flag to be cloned. The key identifies the flag in your code.  For example, setting clone=flagKey will copy the full targeting configuration for all environments (including on/off state) from the original flag to the new flag.
CloneFlagKeyQuery.name: clone

EnvironmentKey.description: The environment key, used to tie together flag configuration and users under one environment so they can be managed together.
EnvironmentKey.name: environmentKey

EnvironmentKeyQuery.description: By default, each feature will include configurations for each environment. You can filter environments with the env query parameter. For example, setting env=["production"] will restrict the returned configurations to just your production environment.
EnvironmentKeyQuery.name: env

EnvironmentMobileKeyExpiry.description: The expiry parameter is deprecated for this endpoint, so the old mobile key will always expire immediately. This parameter will be removed in an upcoming major API client version.
EnvironmentMobileKeyExpiry.name: expiry

EnvironmentSDKKeyExpiry.description: An expiration time for the old environment SDK key, expressed as a Unix epoch time in milliseconds. By default, the key will expire immediately.
EnvironmentSDKKeyExpiry.name: expiry

EvaluationEnvId.description: The environment id for the flag evaluations in question.
EvaluationEnvId.name: envId

EvaluationFlagKey.description: The key of the flag we want metrics for.
EvaluationFlagKey.name: flagKey

EventType.description: The type of event we would like to track.
EventType.name: type

FeatureFlagKey.description: The feature flag's key. The key identifies the flag in your code.
FeatureFlagKey.name: featureFlagKey

FilterQuery.description: A comma-separated list of filters. Each filter is of the form field:value.
FilterQuery.name: filter

HMAC.description: This parameter is required when following "next" links.
HMAC.name: h

IntegrationKey.description: The key used to specify the integration kind.
IntegrationKey.name: integrationKey

LimitQuery.description: The number of objects to return. Defaults to -1, which returns everything.
LimitQuery.name: limit

Offset.description: Specifies the first item to return in the collection.
Offset.name: offset

OffsetQuery.description: Where to start in the list. This is for use with pagination. For example, an offset of 10 would skip the first 10 items and then return the next limit items.
OffsetQuery.name: offset

ProjectKey.description: The project key, used to tie the flags together under one project so they can be managed together.
ProjectKey.name: projectKey

Q.description: Text to search for. You can search for the full or partial name of the resource involved or full or partial email address of the member who made the change.
Q.name: q

RelayProxyConfigsExpiry.description: An expiration time for the old relay proxy configuration key, expressed as a Unix epoch time in milliseconds. By default, the relay proxy configuration will expire immediately
RelayProxyConfigsExpiry.name: expiry

ScheduledChangeId.description: The id of the scheduled change
ScheduledChangeId.name: scheduledChangeId

ScrollId.description: This parameter is required when following "next" links.
ScrollId.name: scrollId

SortQuery.description: A comma-separated list of fields to sort by. A field prefixed by a - will be sorted in descending order.
SortQuery.name: sort

Spec.description: A resource specifier, allowing you to filter audit log listings by resource.
Spec.name: spec

StreamSource.description: The source of where the stream comes from.
StreamSource.name: source

SummaryQuery.description: By default in api version >= 1, flags will _not_ include their list of prerequisites, targets or rules.  Set summary=0 to include these fields for each flag returned.
SummaryQuery.name: summary

Tag.description: Filter by tag. A tag can be used to group flags across projects.
Tag.name: tag

TokenExpiry.description: An expiration time for the old token key, expressed as a Unix epoch time in milliseconds. By default, the token will expire immediately.
TokenExpiry.name: expiry

TokenShowAllQuery.description: If set to true, and the authentication access token has the "Admin" role, personal access tokens for all members will be retrieved.
TokenShowAllQuery.name: showAll

UserSegmentKey.description: The user segment's key. The key identifies the user segment in your code.
UserSegmentKey.name: userSegmentKey

BetaApi403.description: This is a beta API, you must pass beta in the LD-API-Version header to use it.

EvaluationUsage404.description: The environment or flag you requested could not be found

Root200.description: A list of links to available resources in the API.

Standard403.description: Access to the requested resource was denied.

StreamUsage404.description: The stream source you requested could not be found

PatchOperationArray.description: Requires a JSON Patch representation of the desired changes to the project. 'http://jsonpatch.com/'

PatchOperationArray2.description: Requires a JSON Patch representation of the desired changes to the project. 'http://jsonpatch.com/' Feature flag patches also support JSON Merge Patch format. 'https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7386' The addition of comments is also supported.

comment.description: comment will be included in audit log item for change.

description.description: A name that describes the changes you would like to apply to a feature flag configuration

executionDate.description: Timestamp for when instructions will be executed

operatingOnId.description: ID of scheduled change to edit or delete

patchExpiringUserTargetsSemanticpatchwit.description: Requires a Semantic Patch representation of the desired changes to the resource. 'https://apidocs.launchdarkly.com/reference#updates-via-semantic-patches'. The addition of comments is also supported.

appliedByMemberID.description: The id of the member that applied the approval request

appliedDate.description: A unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the date the approval request was applied

creationDate.description: A unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the date the approval request was requested

executionDate.description: Timestamp for when instructions will be executed

operatingOnId.description: ID of scheduled change to edit or delete

requestorId.description: The id of the member that requested the change

status.description:
| Name      | Description |
| ---------:| ----------- |
| pending   | the approval request has not been applied yet |
| completed | the approval request has been applied successfully |
| scheduled | the approval request for a scheduled change has been applied successfully |
| failed    | the approval request has been applied but the changes were not applied successfully |

creationDate.description: A unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the date the approval request was reviewed

ApprovalRequestReviewStatus.description:
| Name     | Description |
| --------:| ----------- |
| pending  | the approval request has not been reviewed yet |
| approved | the approval request has been approved and can now be applied |
| declined | the approval request has been declined and cannot be applied |

description.example: Changed the name from Test to Testing

add.description: Users to add to this list of targets

remove.description: Users to remove from this list of targets

usingEnvironmentId.description: When set to true, this flag will be available to SDKs using the client-side id.

usingMobileKey.description: When set to true, this flag will be available to SDKS using a mobile key.

CustomProperty.description: A name and value describing a custom property.

name.description: The name of the property.

description.example: Description of revenue team role here

key.description: The 20-hexdigit id or the key for a custom role.

name.description: Name of the custom role.

Defaults.description: Default values to be used when a new environment is created.

offVariation.description: The index of the variation to be served when a flag is off.

onVariation.description: The index of the variation to be served when a flag's targeting is on (default variation).

name.description: The destination name

true.description: Whether the data export destination is on or not.

tags.description: An array of tags for this environment.

apiKey.description: The SDK key for backend LaunchDarkly SDKs.

approvalSettings.description: Approval settings for an environment. Only appears if the approvals feature is enabled.

canApplyDeclinedChanges.description: Whether changes can be applied as long as minNumApprovals is met, regardless of if any reviewers have declined a request.

canReviewOwnRequest.description: Whether requesters can approve or decline their own request. They may always comment.

minNumApprovals.description: The number of approvals required before an approval request can be applied.

required.description: Whether any changes to flags in this environment will require approval. You may only set required or requiredApprovalTags, not both.

requiredApprovalTags.description: An array of tags used to specify which flags with those tags require approval. You may only set requiredApprovalTags or required, not both.

color.description: The swatch color for the environment.

confirmChanges.description: Determines if this environment requires confirmation for flag and segment changes.

defaultTrackEvents.description: Set to true to send detailed event information for new flags.

mobileKey.description: The SDK key for mobile LaunchDarkly SDKs.

name.description: The name of the environment.

requireComments.description: Determines if this environment requires comments for flag and segment changes.

secureMode.description: Determines if this environment is in safe mode.

tags.description: An array of tags for this environment.

color.description: A color swatch (as an RGB hex value with no leading '#', e.g. C8C8C8).

confirmChanges.description: Determines if this environment requires confirmation for flag and segment changes.

defaultTrackEvents.description: Set to true to send detailed event information for newly created flags.

defaultTtl.description: The default TTL for the new environment.

key.description: A project-unique key for the new environment.

name.description: The name of the new environment.

requireComments.description: Determines if this environment requires comments for flag and segment changes.

secureMode.description: Determines whether the environment is in secure mode.

tags.description: An array of tags for this feature flag.

archived.description: Whether or not this flag is archived.

archivedDate.description: A unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the archived time of this flag.

creationDate.description: A unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the creation time of this flag.

customProperties.description: A mapping of keys to CustomProperty entries.

description.example: This flag controls whether test feature is turned on or not.

goalIds.description: An array goals from all environments associated with this feature flag

kind.description: Whether the feature flag is a boolean flag or multivariate.

maintainerId.description: The ID of the member that should maintain this flag.

name.description: Name of the feature flag.

temporary.description: Whether or not this flag is temporary.

variations.description: The variations for this feature flag.

trackEvents.description: Set to true to send detailed event information for this flag.

trackEventsFallthrough.description: Set to true to send detailed event information when targeting is enabled but no individual targeting rule is matched.

currentVersion.description: If the latest version of the flag matches provided version it will copy, otherwise it will return a conflict.

key.description: The environment key to be used.

executionDate.description: A unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the date the scheduled changes will be applied

kind.description: The name of the modification you would like to perform on a resource.

name.description:
| Name     | Description |
| --------:| ----------- |
| new      | the feature flag was created within the last 7 days, and has not been requested yet |
| active   | the feature flag was requested by your servers or clients within the last 7 days |
| inactive | the feature flag was created more than 7 days ago, and hasn't been requested by your servers or clients within the past 7 days |
| launched | one variation of the feature flag has been rolled out to all your users for at least 7 days |

| Name     | Description |
| --------:| ----------- |
| new      | the feature flag was created within the last 7 days, and has not been requested yet |
| active   | the feature flag was requested by your servers or clients within the last 7 days |
| inactive | the feature flag was created more than 7 days ago, and hasn't been requested by your servers or clients within the past 7 days |
| launched | one variation of the feature flag has been rolled out to all your users for at least 7 days |

tags.description: An array of tags for this integration configuration.

lastSuccess.description: A unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the last time this integration was successfully used.

config.description: A key-value mapping of configuration fields.

kind.description: The type of integration associated with this configuration.

name.description: The user-defined name associated with this configuration.

true.description: Whether or not the integration is currently active.

_links.description: A mapping of integration types to their respective API endpoints.

_lastSeen.description: A unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the last time this member was active in LaunchDarkly.

tokenId.description: If the last time this member accessed LaunchDarkly was using the REST API, this value will be set to the ID of the personal access token used.

items.description: An action to perform on a resource.

effect.description: Effect of the policy - allow or deny.

notActions.description: Targeted actions will be those actions NOT in this list. The "actions" field must be empty to use this field.

items.description: An action to perform on a resource.

notResources.description: Targeted resource will be those resources NOT in this list. The "resources`" field must be empty to use this field.

tags.description: An array of tags for this project.

creationDate.description: A unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the creation time of this relay proxy configuration

displayKey.description: The last 4 digits of the unique secret key for this relay proxy configuration

fullKey.description: Full secret key. Only included if creating or resetting the relay proxy configuration

lastModified.description: A unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the last time this relay proxy configuration was modified

name.description: A human-friendly name for the relay proxy configuration

_id.description: Feature flag scheduled change id this change will conflict with

reason.description: Feature flag scheduled change conflict reason

kind.description: The name of the modification you would like to perform on a resource.

items.description: An action to perform on a resource.

notActions.description: Targeted actions will be those actions NOT in this list. The "actions" field must be empty to use this field.

items.description: An action to perform on a resource.

notResources.description: Targeted resource will be those resources NOT in this list. The "resources`" field must be empty to use this field.

subseries.description: Links to endpoints that are in the request path.
The following links that are in the response.

0.description: A key corresponding to a time series data point.

time.description: A unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the creation time of this flag.

creationDate.description: A unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the creation time of this access token.

customRoleIds.description: A list of custom role IDs to use as access limits for the access token

defaultApiVersion.description: The default API version for this token

lastModified.description: A unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the last time this access token was modified.

lastUsed.description: A unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the last time this access token was used to authorize access to the LaunchDarkly REST API.

name.description: A human-friendly name for the access token

role.description: The name of a built-in role for the token

serviceToken.description: Whether the token will be a service token https://docs.launchdarkly.com/home/account-security/api-access-tokens#service-tokens

token.description: The last 4 digits of the unique secret key for this access token. If creating or resetting the token, this will be the full token secret.

message.example: This is a beta API, you must pass beta in the LD-API-Version header to use it.

subseries.description: The following links that are in the response.

_value.description: The most important attribute in the response. The _value is the current setting for the user. For a boolean feature toggle, this will be true, false, or null if there is no defined fallthrough value.

setting.description: The setting attribute indicates whether you've explicitly targeted this user to receive a particular variation. For example, if you have explicitly turned off a feature toggle for a user, setting will be false. A setting of null means that you haven't assigned that user to a specific variation.

tags.description: An array of tags for this user segment.

creationDate.description: A unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the creation time of this flag.

description.example: Users in this segment can access beta features.

excluded.description: An array of user keys that should not be included in this segment, unless they are also listed in "included".

included.description: An array of user keys that are included in this segment.

key.description: Unique identifier for the user segment.

name.description: Name of the user segment.

rules.description: An array of rules that can cause a user to be included in this segment.

unbounded.description: Controls whether this is considered a "big segment" which can support an unlimited numbers of users. Include/exclude lists sent with this payload are not used in big segments. Contact your account manager for early access to this feature.

expirationDate.description: Unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the expiration date

variationId.description: the ID of the variation that the user is targeted on a flag

expirationDate.description: Unix epoch time in milliseconds specifying the expiration date

targetType.description: either the included or excluded variation that the user is targeted on a segment

name.description: The name of the webhook.

secret.description: If defined, the webhooks post request will include a X-LD-Signature header whose value will contain an HMAC SHA256 hex digest of the webhook payload, using the secret as the key.

true.description: Whether this webhook is enabled or not.

url.description: The URL of the remote webhook.
